title: daemon/pid.rs: errno not checked after kill(pid,0)
description:
daemon/src/pid.rs process_alive(): calls libc::kill(pid, 0) and returns result == 0. When the PID is owned by a different user, kill returns -1 with errno=EPERM (process exists but caller lacks permission). The current check treats -1 as dead, removes the PID file, and allows a second daemon instance to start — or evicts a PID belonging to an unrelated process.

Root cause: kill(2) has three outcomes: 0 (alive, caller has permission), -1/ESRCH (no such process), -1/EPERM (exists, no permission). Only ESRCH means dead.

Fix: after kill returns -1, read errno; return true (alive) for EPERM, false (dead) only for ESRCH.
